summary: High‑energy Halloween show with standout Werewolves, Shakedown Street and L.L. Rain, captured on a surprisingly clear audience recording.
review: Fans rave about the powerful second set – werewolves jammed hard, shakedown street roared, and the eerie L.L. Rain echo shines – all wrapped in an audience tape that many call crisp and well‑balanced despite occasional vocal drops. The short set (curfew) didn’t dampen the vibe, making this 1985 Halloween gig one of the more memorable recordings from the pre‑coma era.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~4.36/5, with overwhelmingly positive comments about both performance and sound quality; only minor technical quirks are noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Centered mix with strong levels; generally clear and crisp. A few vocal drop‑outs and an exaggerated echo on L.L. Rain are the only blemishes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Shortened second set due to curfew but packed with high‑energy jams; Halloween atmosphere adds extra excitement and audience participation.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as 'all over it' with strong, jam‑filled guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a particularly strong bass performance on this run.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for crazy vocal effects that stood out in the mix.
